mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.4 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20d forward returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 IC over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with rising industrial production indicates robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, which expands corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy by at least 1.5% over any rolling 12-month period, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight capacity utilization, an acceleration in industrial production directly translates to higher equity risk premiums as firms capitalize on pricing power, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are flat or negative when INDPRO growth exceeds 1.0% month-over-month during the specified regime, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.5 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth signals expanding economic activity that boosts corporate revenue expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 3-month correlation between GDPC1 revisions and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.10, or the signal generates negative forward returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity multiples and dampen investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds the threshold, or the predictive Rank IC stays above 0.05 across all rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll growth coupled with rising housing starts indicates synchronized expansion in the services and construction sectors, which fuels consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce a statistically significant positive alpha (t-stat < 1.96) over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are indistinguishable from the market benchmark during high-growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, a decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand that forces margin compression and triggers immediate equity sell-offs, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 50% of triggered instances when RSAFS declines, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 45% over a 2-year walk-forward test.
